groups.name: camunda.process-test.judge
groups.description: Configuration for the AI judge used in agentic process test assertions.

groups.name: camunda.process-test.judge.chat-model
groups.description: Configuration for the chat model used by the AI judge.

groups.name: camunda.process-test.judge.chat-model.credentials
groups.description: AWS credentials for the Amazon Bedrock chat model provider.

properties.name: camunda.process-test.judge.threshold
properties.description: The confidence threshold for the AI judge to consider an assertion as passed.

properties.name: camunda.process-test.judge.custom-prompt
properties.description: A custom prompt to use for the AI judge instead of the default one.

properties.name: camunda.process-test.judge.chat-model.provider
properties.description: The LLM provider to use for the AI judge. Supported providers: openai, anthropic, amazon-bedrock, openai-compatible.

properties.name: camunda.process-test.judge.chat-model.model
properties.description: The model name to use for the AI judge (e.g. 'gpt-4o', 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514', 'us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0').

properties.name: camunda.process-test.judge.chat-model.api-key
properties.description: The API key for authenticating with the chat model provider.

properties.name: camunda.process-test.judge.chat-model.base-url
properties.description: The base URL for the chat model API. Required for the 'openai-compatible' provider.

properties.name: camunda.process-test.judge.chat-model.region
properties.description: The AWS region for the Amazon Bedrock provider (e.g. 'us-east-1').

properties.name: camunda.process-test.judge.chat-model.credentials.access-key
properties.description: The AWS access key for authenticating with Amazon Bedrock.

properties.name: camunda.process-test.judge.chat-model.credentials.secret-key
properties.description: The AWS secret key for authenticating with Amazon Bedrock.

values.description: Use OpenAI models (requires api-key and model).
Use Anthropic models (requires api-key and model).
Use Amazon Bedrock models (requires model; optionally credentials or api-key).
Use an OpenAI-compatible API (requires base-url and model).
